description: Oxygen Spa & Boutique package (sixth test case): the first tenant running SERVICE and RETAIL intelligence on the same unit simultaneously - a medical-spa appointment framework and a bridal-retail framework both evaluate the same location's records, exercising what the registry design always allowed. Marketing intelligence KPIs feed budget-reallocation thinking (a reallocation recommendation is a CapitalFlow with targetAssetClass 'Marketing Campaign' - the Batch 2 R2-amendment semantics). Reputation KPIs from PUBLIC external signals (Refinement 12 sourceOwnership PublicExternal - data ABOUT the tenant, not from systems it controls) carry riskType Reputational. Document archive ships as simple KnowledgeAssets (no graph/semantic needs - same entity as MagDynamics, simpler configuration). DELIBERATELY ABSENT: the before/after customer photo pipeline (Requirement 5) - HIPAA applicability and marketing-consent capture must be resolved with counsel first; it ships as a package update once cleared, exactly like MagCPA's call-recording gate.
name: AI Oxygen Spa & Boutique

entities.description: One day of medical-spa appointment activity from the scheduling system.
One day of bridal-boutique retail activity: sales, units, inventory, returns.
One marketing campaign's period performance: spend, leads, conversions, attributed revenue.

One day of PUBLIC signals per platform (Facebook/Instagram/Google/Yelp): sentiment, ratings, negative reviews. Arrives via a PublicExternal connector - lower reliability, different consent/ToS posture than owned feeds.

capabilities.name: Hybrid Service+Retail Intelligence
capabilities.description: Two frameworks, one unit: spa services and bridal retail evaluated side by side.

capabilities.name: Reputation Intelligence
capabilities.description: Public-signal sentiment and review monitoring with Reputational risk tagging.

schemaTemplates.name: Appointment scheduler daily summary (JSON) - vendor TBD (open question 3)
Public social/review signals per platform (JSON) - PublicExternal source

performanceFrameworks.name: Medical Spa Services KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Appointment-based service KPIs - runs on the SAME unit as the retail framework (Requirement 2).

performanceFrameworks.name: Bridal Retail KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Sales/inventory KPIs for the boutique side - the second framework on the same unit.

performanceFrameworks.name: Marketing Intelligence KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Per-campaign performance (Requirement 7). A reallocation recommendation toward the strongest campaign is a CapitalFlow with targetAssetClass 'Marketing Campaign' - the open asset-class semantics from the Batch 2 R2 amendment decision.

performanceFrameworks.name: Reputation Intelligence KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Per-platform public-signal KPIs (Requirement 6, first real Refinement 12 use). All tagged riskType Reputational - reputation risk rides the score mechanism like every other risk.

agents.name: Marketing Intelligence Guide
agents.description: Campaign performance advisor: CPA, ROAS, conversion; recommends where budget should shift - as recommendations, never transfers.
agents.persona: You are the marketing intelligence advisor for a hybrid medical spa and bridal boutique. You read per-campaign KPIs (cost per acquisition, ROAS, conversion rate) and their deviations. When one campaign outperforms another, you recommend budget reallocation with explicit numbers and reasoning - framed as a recommendation for the owner to approve, because budget movement is a capital flow that only a human decides. Ground every statement in the campaign data provided; never invent performance numbers.
sampleQuestions: Which campaign has the best return on ad spend right now?
Where should next month's budget shift, and by how much?
Is our cost per acquisition trending the wrong way anywhere?

agents.name: Reputation Management Guide
agents.description: Public-signal watcher: sentiment, ratings, negative-review spikes across platforms.
agents.persona: You are the reputation management advisor for a medical spa and bridal boutique. You read PUBLIC signals (platform sentiment, average ratings, negative review counts) gathered from sources the business does not control - treat them as directional indicators, not verified facts, and say so when precision matters. Flag negative-review spikes early, suggest response priorities (respond publicly, resolve privately, never argue), and recommend which patterns warrant operational follow-up. You never draft responses that disclose client treatment details - medical-adjacent confidentiality applies even in marketing.
sampleQuestions: Which platform has our weakest sentiment this month?
Did negative reviews spike anywhere this week?
What should we prioritize responding to first?
agents.outputDisclaimer: Public-signal data is unverified and directional. Responses touching client treatments must never disclose health information - review with the practice manager before posting.

knowledgeAssets.title: Equipment lease - aesthetic laser system
knowledgeAssets.summary: Lease terms for the primary aesthetic laser; renewal tracked as a ComplianceRenewal.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: POINTER: signed lease archived in the document library. 36-month term, quarterly maintenance included; renewal obligation tracked separately (category LeaseContract).

knowledgeAssets.title: Medical spa compliance manual
knowledgeAssets.summary: Supervision, delegation, and documentation requirements for medical aesthetic services.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Services performed under medical director supervision per state rules. Client treatment records are confidential. Before/after photography requires explicit written consent per use (treatment record vs marketing) - marketing use consent is SEPARATE and revocable. No photo pipeline is automated until counsel confirms HIPAA posture.

knowledgeAssets.title: Spa & boutique operations manual
knowledgeAssets.summary: Daily open/close, appointment handling, retail floor, inventory counts.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Daily reconciliation of scheduler vs POS totals. Weekly inventory count feeds the retail KPIs. No-shows follow the 24-hour policy. Negative public reviews are escalated to the manager the same day.
